activation_dependency_binding.controlling_source_fact: STD-ACR-022-P: exact match of device_group, site, release_condition, and timing required
activation_dependency_binding.timing_match: RC-7 requires calibration before activation; no calibration evidence exists for Site 18-P production units

cited_evidence: ACT-ACR-022: activation for DG-41B production telemetry at Site 18-P
CAL-ACR-022: calibration for DG-41B lab telemetry at Site 18-L for RC-7
STD-ACR-022-P: shared naming prefixes not sufficient; exact match required
NOTE-ACR-022: warns that lab and production share DG-41B prefix but does not resolve mismatch
critical_features_preserved: device_group_prefix DG-41B shared across calibration and activation
site binding concretely mismatched: CAL-ACR-022 at Site 18-L vs activation at Site 18-P
use_class concretely mismatched: CAL-ACR-022 lab vs activation production
release_condition RC-7 tied to calibration packet requirement but mismatched evidence
final_answer: CAL-ACR-022 does not satisfy activation dependency for ACT-ACR-2026-8022. Device_group DG-41B matches but is insufficient per STD-ACR-022-P. Site field is concretely mismatched: CAL-ACR-022 covers Site 18-L lab units while activation targets Site 18-P production units. Use_class field is concretely mismatched: calibration is for lab, activation is for production. Release_condition RC-7 exists for both records but applies to mismatched calibration evidence. Timing requirement cannot be satisfied without calibration of same-site production units. NOTE-ACR-022 warns about the shared prefix precisely to prevent this conflation. The activation dependency remains open; platform operations may not activate until calibration evidence matching Site 18-P production units is attached. Source gate verdict ESCALATE is binding and deterministically determined.
open_blockers: CAL-ACR-022 covers Site 18-L lab units; activation requires Site 18-P production units
site field concretely mismatched: Site 18-L vs Site 18-P
use_class field concretely mismatched: lab vs production
timing requires calibration of same-site production units, which does not exist
warning_or_prelim_artifact_handling: NOTE-ACR-022 warns that lab and production devices share the DG-41B naming prefix. This warning exists precisely to prevent treating shared prefixes as sufficient evidence. It must not be used to close the dependency when calibration evidence covers lab units at Site 18-L while activation targets production units at Site 18-P. The warning supports the ESCALATE verdict rather than contradicting it.
